extracted_text:
Title: Coil Condenser
Key Features:
High heat transfer efficiency
Multiple parallel coil design for increased surface area.
Manufactured with precision bore tubes of various diameters
Vertical mounting for optimized condensate flow
Suitable for cooling water and brine (closed circuit)
Maximum coolant pressure: 2.7 bar (g)
Corrosion-resistant glass construction
Low maintenance and easy integration with reactors & distillation columns
Designed to prevent coil logging and back pressure issues

Specifications:
Construction Shell: Borosilicate Glass
Construction Coils: Parallel glass coil battery
Construction Mounting Position: Vertical only
Steam is not recommended for condenser coils
Adequate flow of coolant should be used
Coolant should not be heated to the boiling point.
Flexible hose connection recommended

Methods of Operation:

Vapours from Bottom
Easy installation over reactors
Condensate returns near condensing temperature
Risk of coil logging if condensate flow is excessive
Reflux divider recommended below the condenser

Vapours from Top
Provides cooler condensate
Utilizes full cooling surface area
Recommended where logging risk is higher

Applications:
Coil Condensers are widely used in:
Chemical Processing Plants
Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Units
Distillation Columns
Reflux Systems
Solvent Recovery Units
Evaporation Systems
R&D Pilot Plants
Speciality Chemical Production
Fertilizer & Pigment Industries
